spt_programs.progressionJustification: DUP rotates heavy (3–5 reps), moderate (6–8 reps), and volume (10–12 reps) sessions across the week, providing sufficient mechanical loading for bone density stimulus on heavy days while accumulating hypertrophy volume for sarcopenia prevention on volume days. For a post-menopausal woman with no recent training history, DUP also manages fatigue better than linear progression by varying intensity daily rather than session-to-session.
spt_programs.weeklyVolumeNotes: GLUTES_HAMSTRINGS (11 sets/week) appears in all 3 sessions — hip thrust and RDL anchor two sessions, conventional deadlift anchors the third. UPPER_BACK_ERECTORS (9 sets) spread across all 3 sessions via barbell row, chest-supported row, and cable row primaries/secondaries. QUAD_DOMINANT (9 sets) distributed across Sessions 1 and 3 with back squat primary and goblet squat accessory. PUSH (7 sets) spread across Sessions 2 and 3 via landmine press primary and DB bench press secondary. VERTICAL_PULL (7 sets) across Sessions 1 and 2 via lat pulldown secondary and pull-up/ring row in metcons. UNILATERAL_LOWER (6 sets per leg) spread across all 3 sessions — Bulgarian split squat, step-up, and reverse lunge each appear once. CORE (5 sets) placed last in Sessions 1, 2, and 3 — Pallof press, dead bug, and hollow hold. CARRIES_LOADED present in Session 2 and Session 3 as farmer carry and suitcase carry.

volumeAudit.flag: Count is sets per leg; 3 sets per session × 3 sessions = 9 sets per leg, comfortably above minimum 6

sessions.label: Workout 1 for the week starting July 5, 2026
sessions.focus: Lower Body — Posterior Chain (Heavy DUP) + Vertical Pull

warmup.name: Air bike easy spin
warmup.notes: Low resistance, nasal breathing, warm up hips and ankles

warmup.name: Thoracic spine foam roll
warmup.notes: Pause 3–5 s on stiff segments between shoulder blades; arms crossed on chest

warmup.name: Cat-cow
warmup.notes: Coordinate breath with movement; exhale into flexion, inhale into extension

warmup.name: Band pull-apart
warmup.notes: Scapular retraction; keep elbows soft; light band

warmup.name: Y/T/W raises (prone on bench)
warmup.notes: Thumbs up; squeeze shoulder blades together; use bodyweight only

warmup.name: Hip 90/90 stretch
warmup.notes: Targets hip IR/ER; sit tall, avoid collapsing through lumbar

warmup.name: Glute bridge bodyweight
warmup.notes: Activate glutes before loading; hold top 2 s each rep

warmup.name: Deadlift warm-up ramp
warmup.notes: Groove the hinge pattern; brace before each pull

strength.coachingNotes: Heavy DUP day — 4 sets of 4. With no saved max, begin at 50–55 kg and rate each set RPE 7–8. Bar stays over mid-foot; brace 360° before breaking the floor. Week 2: add 2.5 kg if all reps felt controlled. This is your sole hinge for the primary slot; do not rush the pull.

Non-competing with deadlift — superset as Group A. Start at a load where last 2 reps are challenging but form stays clean, approx 25–35 kg stack. Initiate with depression of scapulae before pulling; address lat tightness by finishing with a 2-second stretch at the top of each rep. Weeks 3–4: aim for 9 reps at same load.

Third exercise in tri-set Group A with deadlift and lat pulldown. Begin with 2 × 8 kg DBs and assess balance and depth; increase to 10 kg if RPE stays ≤7. Front foot flat, rear foot on a low bench. Torso stays upright to load glutes over quads. Count sets per leg for unilateral volume.

Choose a band or cable tension where you feel moderate trunk bracing — light-to-moderate resistance. Do not let hips rotate toward the anchor. Breathe out on the press. Superset with dead bug (Group B) to save time.

Bodyweight. Lower back pressed firmly into floor throughout — if it lifts, shorten the range of motion. Move slowly and breathe out as you extend. Superset with Pallof Press (Group B). Weeks 3–4: add 1 rep per set.

metcon.name: Row & Swing
metcon.description: 14-minute AMRAP. Set up a rower and a KB (16 kg recommended). Each round: 200 m row, then 15 KB swings, then 10 ring rows. Aim for consistent, smooth pacing — not a sprint. Row with strong leg drive. Swings: hinge pattern, squeeze glutes at top, do not hyperextend lumbar. Ring rows: keep body rigid, pull to sternum.

movements.name: Row machine
movements.notes: Moderate damper (3–4); drive with legs first, then lean back, then pull arms

movements.name: KB swing
movements.notes: Russian swing (eye level); hinge — not squat; brace and hinge on the backswing

movements.name: Ring row
movements.notes: Adjust foot position to scale difficulty; body stays plank-like; pull to sternum

mobility.name: Doorway lat stretch (or band-assisted)
mobility.notes: Anchor band or door at shoulder height; step away, hinge at hips, let lat lengthen; breathe into the stretch

mobility.name: Thoracic extension over foam roller
mobility.notes: Segment by segment from T6 to T10; hands behind head, let gravity open the chest

mobility.name: Shoulder internal rotation sleeper stretch
mobility.notes: Side-lying; bottom arm at 90° shoulder/elbow; use top hand to gently press wrist toward floor; stop before pain

mobility.name: Child's pose with lat reach
mobility.notes: Walk hands to one side, breathe into the ribcage; alternate sides halfway through

sessions.label: Workout 2 for the week starting July 5, 2026
sessions.focus: Upper Body — Push + Pull (Moderate DUP) + Hip Thrust

warmup.name: Ski erg easy pull
warmup.notes: Light resistance, focus on lat engagement and thoracic extension; warm up shoulders gently

warmup.name: Thoracic rotation (seated on bench)
warmup.notes: Hands on opposite shoulders; rotate maximally without lumbar compensation

warmup.name: Band pull-apart
warmup.notes: Scapular retraction; light band, focus on rear delt activation

warmup.name: External rotation with band (side-lying or standing)
warmup.notes: Elbow at 90°; rotate forearm up and back; slow and controlled

warmup.name: Scapular push-up
warmup.notes: Protract and retract shoulder blades; elbows locked; serratus anterior activation

warmup.name: Wall slide
warmup.notes: Forearms against wall; slide up while maintaining forearm contact; opens thoracic and shoulder IR

warmup.name: Hip thrust bodyweight
warmup.notes: Drive through heels; squeeze glutes at top; chin tucked; warm up glute-max before loading

strength.coachingNotes: Moderate DUP day — 4 sets of 6 per side. Preferred over barbell overhead press for shoulder safety (avoids extreme shoulder IR loading). Begin with 20–25 kg total bar load; assess with RPE 7–8 target. Keep pressing shoulder packed; slight thoracic extension at lockout to open the chest. Week 2: add 2 kg if technique is solid.

Non-competing with landmine press — superset as Group A. Begin at 40–50 kg; pad the bar over hip crease. Drive through heels, not toes. Full hip extension at top; squeeze for 1 second; do not hyperextend lumbar — rib cage stays down. Weeks 3–4: aim for 10 reps at same load.

Set incline bench to 45°; lie prone. Start with 12–16 kg DBs per hand, RPE 7. Elbows flare slightly (45° from torso) to target mid-trap and rhomboids. Pause at top 1 second. Chest support removes lumbar loading — safe pairing after hip thrust. Weeks 3–4: 11 reps same load.

Load: 16–20 kg per hand — heavy enough that grip is challenged by the end of 40 m but posture stays tall. Shoulders packed down, chin level, core braced. Walk at deliberate pace. Superset with wrist curl (Group C) — grip block combined. Rest 90 s after completing both.

Sit with forearms resting on thighs; use 4–6 kg DBs. Curl wrists fully up and lower slowly. Addresses grip/forearm strength and bone stimulus in the distal radius — important for post-menopausal bone health. Superset with Farmer Carry (Group C).

metcon.name: Ski & Push
metcon.description: 16-minute EMOM, alternating two stations (8 rounds each). Odd minutes (1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11, 13, 15): 12 calories on the ski erg. Even minutes (2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 16): 10 push-ups + 10 ring rows. Scale ski erg calories down to 8 if 12 cals takes more than 50 seconds. Scale push-ups to elevated surface (hands on bench) if needed. Push-pull balance maintained: push-up provides horizontal push, ring row provides horizontal pull.

movements.name: Ski erg
movements.notes: Odd minutes; full arm extension at bottom; engage lats and core; complete within 50 s to allow rest

movements.name: Push-up
movements.notes: Even minutes; scale to elevated surface as needed; full range, chest touches surface

movements.name: Ring row
movements.notes: Even minutes immediately after push-ups; adjust foot position to scale; pull to sternum

mobility.name: Lat doorway or band stretch
mobility.notes: Same as Session 1 — consistent daily lat work to address tightness progressively

mobility.name: Open book thoracic rotation (side-lying)
mobility.notes: Knees stacked at 90°; reach top arm across the body; let thoracic spine rotate; breathe into the stretch

mobility.name: Cross-body shoulder stretch (horizontal adduction)
mobility.notes: Gently pull elbow across chest; targets posterior shoulder and external rotators

mobility.name: Pec minor stretch (hands clasped behind back)
mobility.notes: Lift chest, retract and depress scapulae; counteracts the internal rotation restriction

sessions.label: Workout 3 for the week starting July 5, 2026
sessions.focus: Lower Body — Quad Dominant (Volume DUP) + Row + Posterior Chain

warmup.name: Air bike easy spin
warmup.notes: Warm up legs and cardiovascular system; light resistance

warmup.name: Ankle circles and calf raises
warmup.notes: Prepare ankles for squat depth; elevate heels slightly on plates if dorsiflexion is limited

warmup.name: T-spine rotation with stick (broomstick overhead)
warmup.notes: Hold stick overhead; rotate trunk; keep hips square

warmup.name: Goblet squat bodyweight / light KB
warmup.notes: Use 8 kg KB; elbows in, heels flat; pause 2 s at bottom to open hips and ankles

warmup.name: Single-leg balance (eyes open → eyes closed progression)
warmup.notes: Proprioception activation; stand on one leg, slight knee bend; progress to eyes closed in later weeks

warmup.name: Back squat warm-up ramp
warmup.notes: Groove the movement pattern; elbows up, chest tall, knees tracking over toes

strength.coachingNotes: Volume DUP day — 4 sets of 10. Begin at 40–45 kg; RPE should be 7–8 on the last 3 reps of each set. Depth: aim for hip crease at or below knee. Brace before unracking. Thoracic stiffness note: if bar position is uncomfortable, use a high-bar position or add a pad temporarily while thoracic mobility improves. Week 2: add 2.5 kg if all 4 × 10 are completed cleanly.

Non-competing pattern with back squat — superset as Group A. Begin at 40–45 kg; soft knee bend; hinge until bar passes knees and you feel a hamstring stretch. Do not round lower back. This is one of maximum 2 hinge movements this session (this is the only hinge secondary here). Weeks 3–4: 11 reps at same load.
Third exercise in tri-set Group A. Neutral grip; sit tall; initiate with scapular retraction before arm pull; pause 1 s at the ribcage. Begin at 30–40 kg stack; RPE 7. Weeks 3–4: 11 reps same load.

Use a 30–35 cm box and 8–10 kg DBs. Drive through the heel of the working leg; drive the opposite knee up at the top with power intent — this is the low-impact plyometric element for the week. Lower slowly with control. Superset with suitcase carry (Group B) — unilateral pairing.

Load: 16–20 kg KB in one hand. Anti-lateral-flexion core demand — resist tilting toward the loaded side. Keep shoulders level, walk tall. Alternate hands each set. Superset with step-up (Group B) to save time.

Bodyweight. Lower back pressed into floor; arms overhead or by hips — start with arms by hips or knees bent to scale. Build to full hold with arms overhead by Week 4. Breathe shallow; do not let lower back arch off the floor. Placed last — core finisher.

metcon.name: The Tripler
metcon.description: 5 rounds for time (15-minute cap). Each round: 12 goblet squats, 10 med ball slams, 8 pull-ups (or ring rows as scale). Goblet squats provide quad stimulus reinforcing the day's primary. Med ball slams are the low-impact power/plyometric element. Pull-ups provide vertical pull to balance the push from Session 2. Rest as needed between rounds — this is not a sprint.

movements.name: Goblet squat
movements.notes: Hold KB at chest; heels flat; full depth; keep torso upright

movements.name: Med ball slam
movements.notes: Reach fully overhead, then slam hard into the floor; catch or pick up for next rep; do not hyperextend lumbar at the top

movements.name: Pull-up
movements.notes: Use band assistance if needed; full hang at bottom, chin over bar at top; scale to ring row if no band available

mobility.name: Lat stretch with foam roller (side-lying)
mobility.notes: Lie on side; arm extended overhead onto roller; let body weight gently lengthen the lat

mobility.name: Thoracic extension over foam roller
mobility.notes: Segment T6–T10; hold each segment 5–10 s; arms crossed or hands behind head

mobility.name: Shoulder IR stretch — sleeper stretch
mobility.notes: Side-lying; gently press wrist toward floor; hold at first point of resistance; breathe

mobility.name: Hip flexor lunge stretch
mobility.notes: Low lunge; tuck pelvis under; reach ipsilateral arm overhead to open hip flexor and lat simultaneously

sessions.label: Workout 4 for the week starting July 12, 2026
sessions.focus: Lower Body — Posterior Chain (Heavy DUP) + Vertical Pull

warmup.name: Air bike easy spin
warmup.notes: Warm up hips and posterior chain

warmup.name: Thoracic spine foam roll
warmup.notes: T6–T10 segment by segment; pause on stiff spots

warmup.name: Y/T/W raises (prone on bench)
warmup.notes: Bodyweight; rotator cuff and lower trap activation

warmup.name: Hip 90/90 stretch
warmup.notes: Hip IR/ER mobility prep for deadlift setup

warmup.name: Deadlift ramp
warmup.notes: Approach working weight with controlled warm-up sets

strength.coachingNotes: Week 2 heavy DUP — add 2.5 kg from Week 1 if all sets felt RPE ≤8 last session. Target RPE 8 on the final set. Maintain the same technical cues: mid-foot bar path, 360° brace, drive floor away. If form breaks on Rep 4 of any set, stop that set and note it for load adjustment.

Week 2: same load as Week 1; add 1 rep (target 9 reps) on Sets 3 and 4 if they felt easy last week. Superset Group A with deadlift — rest period of the deadlift is used to set up for lat pulldown. Initiate each rep with lat depression before arm bend.
Week 2: add 1–2 kg per DB if Week 1 felt RPE ≤7. Maintain upright torso; front foot flat; controlled descent. Group A tri-set continues. Count sets per leg for unilateral volume target.
Same load as Week 1 (establish working tension at RPE 7). Superset with dead bug (Group B). Week 3: add 1 rep (13 per side) at same tension.
Week 2: same reps; focus on control and breath. Lower back flat throughout. Superset with Pallof Press (Group B). Week 3: 11 reps per side.

metcon.name: Swing & Row Ladder
metcon.description: For time (14-min cap): 5 rounds of descending KB swings (20-16-12-8-4) paired with 250 m row each round. Round 1: 20 KB swings + 250 m row. Round 2: 16 KB swings + 250 m row. Round 3: 12 swings + 250 m row. Round 4: 8 swings + 250 m row. Round 5: 4 swings + 250 m row. The descending swing ladder allows you to push harder on each round as fatigue accumulates. Push/pull balance: row machine provides the pull component.

movements.name: KB swing
movements.notes: Russian swing; hinge pattern; brace on backswing; do not hyperextend at top

movements.name: Row machine
movements.notes: Consistent splits; drive with legs; avoid jerking the handle

mobility.name: Doorway lat stretch (band-assisted)
mobility.notes: Consistent daily lat work

mobility.name: Thoracic extension over foam roller
mobility.notes: T6–T10; gravity-assisted extension

mobility.name: Sleeper stretch (shoulder IR)
mobility.notes: Do not force — move to first point of resistance only

mobility.name: Child's pose with lat reach
mobility.notes: Alternate side reach every 30 s

sessions.label: Workout 5 for the week starting July 12, 2026
sessions.focus: Upper Body — Push + Pull (Moderate DUP) + Hip Thrust

warmup.name: Ski erg easy pull
warmup.notes: Shoulder and lat warm-up; light resistance

warmup.name: Thoracic rotation (seated on bench)
warmup.notes: Rotate around mid-spine; keep hips still

warmup.name: External rotation with band
warmup.notes: 90° elbow; slow external rotation; rotator cuff warm-up

warmup.name: Wall slide
warmup.notes: Forearms on wall; slide up; thoracic extension

strength.coachingNotes: Week 2: add 2 kg from Week 1 if all sets felt RPE ≤8. Technical focus this week: at the top of the press, pause 1 second and actively push the shoulder blade into protraction (serratus activation) before lowering. This helps counteract the internal rotation restriction.

Week 2: add 5 kg from Week 1 if all sets felt comfortable. Hip thrust is non-competing with landmine press — superset Group A. Rib cage down; drive heels; 1-second squeeze at top. This is your second hip thrust appearance this week (with Session 4 having deadlifts as primary glute work).

Week 2: same load as Week 1; aim for 11 reps on Sets 2 and 3 if Week 1 felt RPE ≤7. 45° incline bench; elbows 45° from torso; pause at top. Standalone Group B — not supersetted this week to allow upper back full recovery between sets.
Week 2: same load as Week 1 (16–20 kg per hand) or add 2 kg if grip felt easy. Shoulders packed; tall posture; deliberate pace. Superset with reverse wrist curl (Group C) — grip block.

Week 2 forearm variation — reverse curl trains the extensors (complement to Week 1's wrist curl which trained flexors). Use 3–5 kg DB. Forearms on thighs, palms down; extend wrists fully up and lower slowly. Superset with farmer carry (Group C).

metcon.name: Air Bike Chipper
metcon.description: For time (15-min cap): Complete in order — 40 calories on the air bike, then 30 push-ups, then 30 ring rows, then 20 calories air bike, then 20 push-ups, then 20 ring rows. Scale push-ups to elevated surface if needed. Ring row difficulty can be adjusted by foot placement. This is a push/pull balanced chipper with a VO₂ cardio element on the air bike.

movements.name: Air bike
movements.notes: Steady hard effort; use arms and legs; avoid redlining — you need to push-up after

movements.name: Push-up
movements.notes: Break into manageable sets (e.g. 5–10 reps); scale to bench if needed

movements.name: Ring row
movements.notes: Pull to sternum; adjust foot position for scaling; rigid body

movements.name: Air bike
movements.notes: Push pace on this set — shorter distance, more effort

movements.name: Push-up
movements.notes: Same scaling as above

movements.name: Ring row
movements.notes: Finish strong; control the descent

mobility.name: Lat doorway or band stretch
mobility.notes: Consistent daily lat work

mobility.name: Open book thoracic rotation (side-lying)
mobility.notes: Let the thoracic spine follow the arm; breathe into the open side

mobility.name: Cross-body shoulder stretch
mobility.notes: Posterior shoulder and capsule stretch

mobility.name: Pec minor stretch (hands clasped behind back)
mobility.notes: Chest tall; scapulae retracted and depressed

sessions.label: Workout 6 for the week starting July 12, 2026
sessions.focus: Lower Body — Quad Dominant (Volume DUP) + Row + Posterior Chain

warmup.name: Goblet squat with 8 kg KB
warmup.notes: Pause 2 s at bottom; heels flat; warm up ankles and hips

warmup.name: Single-leg balance — eyes closed progression
warmup.notes: Week 2: attempt eyes closed for 15 s; bend knee slightly; build proprioception

strength.coachingNotes: Week 2: add 2.5 kg from Week 1 if all sets were completed cleanly at RPE ≤8. Focus this week on tempo: 3-second descent, 1-second pause at bottom, drive up. Tempo squats at higher volume build tremendous quad hypertrophy and bone loading.
Week 2: add 2.5 kg if Week 1 felt RPE ≤7. Non-competing with squat — superset Group A. Keep bar close to legs; hinge until hamstring stretch is felt; pause 1 s. Weeks 3–4: 11 reps.
Week 2: add 5 kg stack if Week 1 felt RPE ≤7. Maintain tall posture; no momentum; pause at the ribcage. Tri-set Group A with squat and RDL continues.

Week 2: add 1–2 kg per DB if Week 1 felt controlled. Explosive drive through heel; opposing knee drives up; slow eccentric. Superset with suitcase carry (Group B). Low-impact power element for the week.
Week 2: same load as Week 1 or add 2 kg if posture stayed perfect. Resist lateral lean. Superset with step-up (Group B).
Week 2: add 5 s from Week 1 (25 s → 30 s). Same form cues: lower back flat, breathe shallow. Weeks 3–4: progress arm position overhead if not yet achieved.

metcon.name: Goblet Grinder
metcon.description: 12-minute AMRAP. Each round: 10 goblet squats, 12 med ball slams, 8 ring rows, 10 step-ups (5 per leg on a 30 cm box). Consistent pacing — aim for the same round time each round. Note the rounds completed at the 12-minute mark. Push/pull: ring rows provide the pull component against the slam (power) and squat.

movements.name: Goblet squat
movements.notes: Full depth; heels flat; torso upright

movements.name: Med ball slam
movements.notes: Reach fully overhead; slam hard; pick up quickly; low-impact power element

movements.name: Ring row
movements.notes: Rigid body; pull to sternum; adjust foot position to scale

movements.name: Step-up
movements.notes: 30 cm box; alternate legs; controlled descent; no hard landing

mobility.name: Lat stretch with foam roller (side-lying)
mobility.notes: Let body weight lengthen the lat; breathe into the stretch

mobility.name: Thoracic extension over foam roller
mobility.notes: T6–T10; pause on stiff segments

mobility.name: Sleeper stretch
mobility.notes: Shoulder IR mobility; first point of resistance only

mobility.name: Hip flexor lunge stretch
mobility.notes: Tuck pelvis; reach overhead; combined hip flexor and lat release

sessions.label: Workout 7 for the week starting July 19, 2026
sessions.focus: Lower Body — Posterior Chain (Heavy DUP) + Vertical Pull

warmup.name: Row machine easy pull
warmup.notes: Posterior chain warm-up; low damper; nasal breathing

strength.coachingNotes: Week 3 heavy day — add 2.5 kg from Week 2. Intensity is now ~82% and you should feel genuine effort on the last rep of each set (RPE 8–9 set 4). Reset your brace between every rep (touch and go is acceptable but dead-stop preferred for bone stimulus). If the top set feels above RPE 9, hold the load for one more week before adding.

Week 3: add 1 rep (9 reps) per set at same load as Weeks 1–2 — accessory progression rule. Superset Group A with deadlift. Lat tightness note: at the top of each rep, let the weight pull your arms fully overhead and hold 2 seconds to stretch the lat under load.

Week 3: accessory variation — switching from Bulgarian split squat to reverse lunge to rotate accessory selection (per cycle rotation rule). Begin with 10–12 kg DBs; step back far enough that front shin stays vertical. Controlled descent; drive through front heel to stand. Count sets per leg.
Week 3: add 1 rep (13 per side) at same tension — accessory progression. Superset with dead bug (Group B).
Week 3: add 1 rep (11 per side) — accessory progression. Same form cues apply. Superset with Pallof Press (Group B).

metcon.name: Hinge & Row Intervals
metcon.description: 15-minute EMOM — 3 movements rotating every minute. Minute 1: 15 KB swings. Minute 2: 200 m row machine (pace firmly). Minute 3: 10 ring rows. Repeat 5 rounds (15 minutes total). The row machine provides VO₂ stimulus; KB swings add posterior chain volume; ring rows balance the push/pull. All low-impact.

movements.name: KB swing
movements.notes: Minute 1 of each round; Russian swing; hinge pattern

movements.name: Row machine
movements.notes: Minute 2; pace at ~80% effort; complete within 55 s to allow brief rest

movements.name: Ring row
movements.notes: Minute 3; pull to sternum; rigid body

mobility.name: Doorway lat stretch (band-assisted)
mobility.notes: Consistent daily lat work

mobility.name: Thoracic extension over foam roller
mobility.notes: Gravity-assisted T-spine extension

mobility.name: Sleeper stretch (shoulder IR)
mobility.notes: First point of resistance; breathe

mobility.name: Child's pose with lat reach
mobility.notes: Alternate sides every 30 s

sessions.label: Workout 8 for the week starting July 19, 2026
sessions.focus: Upper Body — Push + Pull (Moderate DUP) + Hip Thrust

warmup.name: Wall slide
warmup.notes: Thoracic extension and shoulder IR opener

strength.coachingNotes: Week 3: add 2 kg from Week 2. You are now 4+ kg heavier than Week 1 on this lift. Focus on shoulder packing and slight thoracic extension at lockout. RPE target: 8 on the final set. If Week 2 was smooth, add the full 2 kg; if it was a grind, add 1 kg only.
Week 3: add 1 rep (10 reps) at same load as Weeks 1–2 — accessory progression rule. This is the rep increase phase (Weeks 3–4). Superset Group A with landmine press.

Week 3 rotation: switching from chest-supported row to single-arm DB row for accessory variation. Use a bench for support; begin with 14–18 kg DB; RPE 7. Pull elbow to hip, not shoulder; pause 1 s at top. Weeks 3–4: add 1 rep per set (11 reps next session).
Week 3: add 2 kg per hand if posture was perfect in Weeks 1–2, or maintain same load for one more week. Superset with rice bucket drill (Group C) — grip variation this week.

Third grip variation — use a bucket of rice (or sand); plunge hand in and open/close fingers, rotate wrist, and grip in multiple directions for 30 s each hand. Builds grip endurance and forearm health differently from wrist curls. Superset with farmer carry (Group C).

metcon.name: Push & Pull Engine
metcon.description: 4 rounds for time (14-min cap). Each round: 15 push-ups, 15 pull-ups (or ring rows), 10 DB thrusters. Push-ups and pull-ups provide the horizontal push/pull balance. DB thrusters add an overhead push element (1 overhead movement for the metcon) with quad and glute demand. Scale push-ups to elevated surface, scale pull-ups to ring rows with feet on floor.

movements.name: Push-up
movements.notes: Scale to elevated surface as needed; full range of motion

movements.name: Pull-up
movements.notes: Use band assistance; scale to ring row; pull to chin over bar

movements.name: DB thruster
movements.notes: Sole overhead movement this metcon; full squat depth; press to lockout at top; shoulders packed

mobility.name: Lat doorway or band stretch
mobility.notes: Consistent daily lat work

mobility.name: Open book thoracic rotation (side-lying)
mobility.notes: Thoracic rotation; breathe into open side

mobility.name: Cross-body shoulder stretch
mobility.notes: Posterior shoulder and capsule

mobility.name: Pec minor stretch
mobility.notes: Chest tall; scapulae retracted and depressed

sessions.label: Workout 9 for the week starting July 19, 2026
sessions.focus: Lower Body — Quad Dominant (Volume DUP) + Row + Posterior Chain

warmup.name: Goblet squat with 10 kg KB
warmup.notes: Slightly heavier than Weeks 1–2 to warm up at approaching load

warmup.name: Single-leg balance — eyes closed
warmup.notes: Week 3: maintain full 30 s eyes closed; slight perturbation if comfortable

strength.coachingNotes: Week 3: add one extra set (5 sets total) at same load as Week 2 if all 4 sets were RPE ≤8. This volume accumulation is the primary bone-density driver for quads. Tempo cue: 3-second descent, brief pause, drive up. If fatigue accumulates, drop Set 5 to 8 reps — form first.
Week 3: add 1 rep (11 reps) at same load — accessory progression. Superset Group A with squat and cable row. Hinge until hamstring stretch; pause 1 s; no lumbar rounding.
Week 3: add 1 rep (11 reps) at same load — accessory progression. Tri-set Group A continues. Initiate with scapular retraction; pause at ribcage 1 s.
Week 3: add 1 rep (9 per leg) at same DB load — accessory progression. Explosive drive; opposing knee drive at top; slow eccentric. Superset with suitcase carry (Group B).
Week 3: same load or add 2 kg if posture was perfect last two weeks. Anti-lateral-flexion core demand. Superset with step-up (Group B).
Week 3: add 5 s from Week 2 (30 s → 35 s). Arms should be extended overhead by now if Week 2 felt manageable. Lower back flat throughout.

metcon.name: Volume Finisher
metcon.description: 12-minute AMRAP. Same structure as Session 6 (Week 2) to allow direct comparison and track fitness improvement. Each round: 10 goblet squats, 12 med ball slams, 8 ring rows, 10 step-ups (5 per leg). Compare total rounds to Week 2 Session 6. A meaningful improvement is 0.5–1 full extra round.

movements.name: Goblet squat
movements.notes: Full depth; heels flat; elbows in

movements.name: Med ball slam
movements.notes: Reach overhead fully; slam with power; pick up quickly

movements.name: Ring row
movements.notes: Pull to sternum; rigid body

movements.name: Step-up
movements.notes: Controlled; no hard landing; 30 cm box

mobility.name: Lat stretch with foam roller (side-lying)
mobility.notes: Body weight lengthens the lat

mobility.name: Thoracic extension over foam roller
mobility.notes: T6–T10 segment by segment

mobility.name: Sleeper stretch
mobility.notes: Shoulder IR; first point of resistance

mobility.name: Hip flexor lunge stretch with overhead reach
mobility.notes: Combined hip flexor and lat release

sessions.label: Workout 10 for the week starting July 26, 2026
sessions.focus: Lower Body — Posterior Chain (Heavy DUP) + Vertical Pull — Peak Week

strength.coachingNotes: Week 4 — peak heavy day: drop to 3 reps per set, increase load by 2.5 kg from Week 3. This is your highest-intensity deadlift of the cycle. RPE 8–9 on final set. Do NOT push to technical failure — if bar speed slows significantly on Rep 2, terminate the set. This near-maximal loading is the primary bone density stimulus. Full 4-minute rest between sets if needed.

Week 4: maintain the 9 reps from Week 3; increase load by 2–3 kg on the stack — this is the load reset phase transition. Superset Group A with deadlift. Two-second stretch at the top to maintain lat mobility work.
Week 4: add 1 rep (9 per leg) at same DB load as Week 3 — accessory progression. Controlled descent; front shin vertical; drive through front heel. Group A tri-set continues.
Week 4: same 13 reps as Week 3; increase tension slightly (one notch on cable or shorter band) if RPE felt below 7. Superset with dead bug (Group B).
Week 4: maintain 11 reps; focus on maximum control and slow tempo (3 s per limb extension). Superset with Pallof Press (Group B).

metcon.name: Peak Week Power Row
metcon.description: For time (15-min cap): 5 rounds of 12 KB swings, 15 sit-ups, 250 m row. Sit-ups add core flexion volume. Row provides the VO₂ pull stimulus. KB swings maintain posterior chain engagement in the metcon. This is a moderate-effort peak-week metcon — not a max effort, as heavy deadlifts already provided peak neural demand today.

movements.name: KB swing
movements.notes: Russian swing; hinge pattern; brace on backswing; glute squeeze at top

movements.name: Sit-up
movements.notes: Anchor feet; controlled ascent and descent; arms across chest or reaching overhead

movements.name: Row machine
movements.notes: Steady pace; strong leg drive; avoid redlining after heavy deadlift session

mobility.name: Doorway lat stretch (band-assisted)
mobility.notes: Extended duration this week — cumulative lat work across 4 weeks should begin showing improved range; hold longer

mobility.name: Thoracic extension over foam roller
mobility.notes: Gravity-assisted T-spine opening

mobility.name: Sleeper stretch
mobility.notes: Week 4: hold slightly longer to consolidate IR gains

mobility.name: Child's pose with lat reach
mobility.notes: Alternate sides; breathe into ribcage

sessions.label: Workout 11 for the week starting July 26, 2026
sessions.focus: Upper Body — Push + Pull (Moderate DUP) + Hip Thrust — Peak Week

strength.coachingNotes: Week 4 peak — reduce reps to 5, increase load by 2 kg. This is the heaviest landmine press of the cycle. RPE 8–9 on the final set. Press with maximal intent; pause at lockout 1 s; full shoulder pack. If the final set feels compromised, drop 1 rep rather than losing form.

Week 4: maintain 10 reps; add 5 kg from Week 3 — entering the load reset progression phase (Weeks 3–4 add load). This is the heaviest hip thrust of the cycle. Keep rib cage down; full hip extension; 1-second squeeze. Superset Group A with landmine press.
Week 4: add 1 rep (11 reps) at same load as Week 3 — accessory progression. Pull to hip; pause 1 s at top. Standalone Group B.

Week 4: add 2 kg per hand from Week 3 (peak carry load this cycle). Shoulders packed; walk with purpose; grip should be challenged by 30 m mark. Superset with balance drill (Group C) — balance element for the 2-week mandatory block.

Stand on one leg on a slightly unstable surface (foam pad or BOSU flat side up if available, or simply eyes closed on the floor). Have a partner or coach gently perturb the hip/shoulder unpredictably; react and stabilise. If no partner available, stand eyes closed on foam pad. This proprioception work supports fall prevention and joint health. Superset with farmer carry (Group C).

metcon.name: Ski & Rings Finisher
metcon.description: Tabata-style: 8 rounds of 20 s on / 10 s off for each of two exercises, completing all 8 rounds of one before moving to the next. Exercise 1: ski erg (8 rounds × 20 s max effort). Rest 2 minutes. Exercise 2: ring rows (8 rounds × 20 s max reps). Total time ~16 min. No skill or overhead movements in Tabata — per metcon hard constraint. Push/pull balance: ski erg engages pulling muscles; ring rows provide horizontal pull.

movements.name: Ski erg
movements.notes: 8 rounds × 20 s; max effort each interval; full arm extension at bottom; engage lats; rest 10 s between rounds

movements.name: Ring row
movements.notes: After 2-min rest: 8 rounds × 20 s max reps; pull to sternum; adjust foot position; rigid body

mobility.name: Lat doorway or band stretch
mobility.notes: Extended duration Week 4 — consolidate gains

mobility.name: Open book thoracic rotation (side-lying)
mobility.notes: Thoracic rotation; breathe into open side

mobility.name: Cross-body shoulder stretch
mobility.notes: Posterior shoulder; extended hold Week 4

mobility.name: Pec minor stretch
mobility.notes: Chest tall; scapulae retracted and depressed

sessions.label: Workout 12 for the week starting July 26, 2026
sessions.focus: Lower Body — Quad Dominant (Volume DUP) + Row + Posterior Chain — Peak Week

warmup.name: Goblet squat with 12 kg KB
warmup.notes: Heavier than Week 1 warm-up to reflect improved strength; pause 2 s at bottom

warmup.name: Single-leg balance with perturbation
warmup.notes: Eyes closed on foam pad; reinforce balance work from Session 11

strength.coachingNotes: Week 4 peak volume day — 5 sets of 10 at the heaviest volume load of the cycle. Add 2.5 kg from Week 3. This session creates the highest cumulative mechanical load for quad and bone stimulus. Maintain 3-second tempo on descent. If Set 4 or 5 drops below 8 clean reps, reduce load by 5 kg rather than grinding with poor form. End-of-cycle benchmark: note the total load lifted today.

Week 4: maintain 11 reps from Week 3; add 2.5 kg — load reset progression phase. Non-competing with squat — superset Group A. Keep form pristine: bar close, soft knees, hamstring stretch before reversing.
Week 4: maintain 11 reps; add 5 kg stack. Tri-set Group A continues. Scapular retraction first; pause at ribcage. Peak load on cable row this cycle.
Week 4: maintain 9 reps per leg; add 2 kg per DB — this is the load reset phase. Explosive intent on each step; slow and controlled lowering. Superset with suitcase carry (Group B).
Week 4: add 2 kg from Week 3. This is the heaviest suitcase carry of the cycle. Resist lateral lean aggressively. Superset with step-up (Group B).

Week 4: add 5 s from Week 3 (35 s → 40 s). Arms fully extended overhead; lower back flat; this is the benchmark hollow hold duration to carry into the next 4-week block. Note your quality and use it as a baseline.

metcon.name: End-of-Cycle Benchmark
metcon.description: For time (15-min cap) — same workout as Week 1 Session 1 metcon (Row & Swing): 200 m row + 15 KB swings + 10 ring rows, repeated for as many rounds as possible in 14 minutes, OR complete the same number of rounds as Week 1 and compare your finish time. Use this as a direct fitness benchmark. Any improvement in rounds or time is measurable progress after 4 weeks.

movements.name: Row machine
movements.notes: Same benchmark as Week 1 Session 1; compare pacing and effort level

movements.name: KB swing
movements.notes: Russian swing; if 16 kg felt easy across the cycle, attempt 20 kg for 2–3 rounds and note it

movements.name: Ring row
movements.notes: Pull to sternum; note whether the same foot position feels easier than Week 1

mobility.name: Lat stretch with foam roller (side-lying)
mobility.notes: Extended duration — Week 4 consolidation; note any improvement in range vs Week 1

mobility.name: Thoracic extension over foam roller
mobility.notes: T6–T10; assess whether stiff segments feel different after 4 weeks of consistent work

mobility.name: Sleeper stretch
mobility.notes: Week 4 extended hold; note any improvement in shoulder IR range

mobility.name: Hip flexor lunge stretch with overhead reach
mobility.notes: Combined hip flexor and lat release; breathe fully; note any changes in range from Week 1
